title: Chapter 29
text: Chapter Twenty - Six R onin brushed his fingers over Lara’s cheek and watched her lashes flutter open. She stretched against him and released a long, slow breath. Turning toward him, she stared up through the darkness, pupils dilated . “Is it time to go?” she rasped . “After you have something to eat and drink .” “ Kay .” Lara pulled herself out of bed with a soft grunt. Ronin moved ahead of her, lighting the lantern. She raised her hand to shield her eyes from the light, and yawned into the crook of her elbow . “How long was I asleep ?” “Five hours and twelve minutes. The sun should be up by now .” “How long did the storm last?” She found her canteen and took a long drink . “We last heard it a little more than three hours ago,” Newton replied from his seat on the steps. “It was one of the more tenacious storms in recent memory .” Ronin checked the straps and ties on their bags. He’d wanted to be days away from Cheyenne before making such a long stop, but the Dust didn’t care about anyone’s plans. They had miles to make up; Warlord was far too close for Ronin’s liking . “The toilet is through there,” Newton said, gesturing to the closed door near Lara. “I’ve kept it in working condition, for lack of anything better to do .” “Seriously? I’ve been holding it this whole time only for you to tell me you have a toilet now ?” “My apologies. Given the nature of our earlier conversations, there seemed no prudent opportunities to inform you .” “Any time is a good time to tell someone there’s a toilet.” She smirked before rushing into the room and closing the door behind her . “I will have to keep that in mind for future encounters,” Newton mused. “She is uniquely spirited, Ronin .” You should see her dance . He stopped himself from saying it aloud. Lara’s dancing, Ronin decided, was for him. “Learned more about being alive in a few weeks with her than I have since you reactivated me .” “Would that couplings such as yours were the rule, rather than the exception to it .” Ronin shrugged. He spread a cloth on the floor and laid his rifle atop it, disassembling it rapidly. “Doesn’t make much difference what anyone else says or does. The world’s approval doesn’t matter to us .” “You would face deactivation for her, if it came down to it?” There was a strange hesitance in Newton’s voice . “Yes. I’ve faced it over things far less important to me than she is .” The door opened, and Lara emerged. Her face was scrubbed clean, cheeks pink, and she’d wrapped her scarf over her hair . “You have no idea how much better I feel, now.” She moved to her pack, closed it, and swung it up over her shoulders. “I’ll eat on the way .” “All right.” Ronin inspected the rifle’s parts closely. They’d made it out of the storm before much dust had built up inside of it, so he cleaned the pieces quickly and put them back together. That done, he stood up and pulled on the other bags . Lara approached Newton. He stood as she neared . “Thank you. For letting us stay .” “It is the least I could do,” Newton replied, lip plates lifting into a smile. “Especially considering you are the ones with the firearms .” She returned his smile, though her expression slipped a moment later. “I’m…sorry. For what I said. I know there isn’t — ” “You do not need to apologize to me. You’ve every right to be angry about what’s happened, and there’s nothing we can say or do to erase those wrongs. I wish we’d met under more pleasant circumstances, Miss Brooks, but I am glad to have met you, all the same .” “Me too,” she said. “Maybe…you could come with us? I mean, this place is better than my old shack, but…you could do better .” “Perhaps the next time you stumble in unannounced, I will accept the invitation,” he replied, a sad humor in his voice. “I don’t think I’m quite ready to lift my self-imposed exile, but you’ve given me much to think on .” “Well, then, I guess…until next time ?” Newton stood aside to allow her up the stairs . Ronin stopped in front of Newton. “ Thank you .” Newton nodded. “The name you’ve chosen suits you well. Do not lose who you have become .” “I won’t,” Ronin replied, “and I hope you remember who you were, before too much longer .” Lara jiggled the broken handle and pushed the heavy door open. The metal-on-metal scrape of its hinges was like the groan of a dying animal, echoing off the concrete walls . The next sound, so small in comparison but so much more powerful, was Lara’s startled gasp. Ronin swung his optics upward to see hands closing on her arms, and then she was dragged outside his field of vision . “Ronin!” she screamed . He leapt up the stairs and burst into the yellow-gray morning, swinging his rifle into hand. Lara was near the house. Ronin didn’t waste any processing power contemplating how it had withstood the storm . Two gearheads held Lara, one with a large hand clamped over her mouth. Warlord stood beside them. Ronin took aim at him, but didn’t fire. The risk to Lara was too great . Wordlessly, the gearheads shifted Lara to Warlord’s hold. He wrapped one arm around her neck and the other around her waist, cutting off her scream by putting pressure on her throat. Her fingers clawed at his forearm. Wide-eyed, she met Ronin’s optics . “Put the gun down, dustwalker,” Warlord commanded flatly . Ronin hesitated, simulations running frantically, searching for some way to turn the situation to his favor. To save her . “You disobey, and you know how it ends. Don’t make me ask again .” Hovering near the trigger guard, Ronin’s finger twitched. Would one shot be enough to end it? To do what Lara wanted, to end Warlord for good, and free all the people who lived in his shadow of terror? Was this the sort of sacrifice she wanted them to make ? But he couldn’t risk her . He removed his left hand from the foregrip and crouched, reaching forward to place the rifle on the dusty ground . Warlord’s optics flicked infinitesimally from side to side. Ronin’s audio receptors picked up a whisper of grass, and then a great weight hit him from each side. Two more gearheads. They took hold of his arms, wrenching them behind his back, pulled off his packs, and forced him facedown into the dirt . “I prefer it when things go the easy way,” Warlord said as one of the other gearheads strode forward and plucked up the rifle. “This part was easy. Unfortunately, you made the rest of this very unpleasant for me. I don’t appreciate having to leave my city because my leniency’s been abused .” “You said to get rid of her. She’s not in Cheyenne anymore.” Ronin twisted his head to center Lara in his vision . “I did, didn’t I? I shouldn’t be surprised you took it that way. Whenever I think I’ve made something abundantly clear to you, it goes over your head. Guess I’ll just have to demonstrate what I mean when I tell you to get rid of something .” Warlord released Lara only long enough to clamp a hand on the back of her neck. His lip curled as he looked her over. “You should have stayed in the dirt, where you belong.” He slammed a fist into her abdomen . Lara doubled over, wheezing, and crumpled at Warlord’s feet. She convulsed and vomited, emptying the meager contents of her stomach onto the ground . Ronin’s processors went into overdrive, pouring power into his actuators for two tasks — destroying his enemies and protecting Lara. He surged up, and the gearheads restraining him stumbled away. One caught Ronin’s wrist as he started toward Warlord . Spinning, Ronin slammed his fist down on the bot’s outstretched arm, hitting its elbow joint from the side. Metal crunched as it bent in the wrong direction, breaking the gearhead’s hold. It was enough of a delay for the second gearhead to recover . The other bot slammed into Ronin from the side, knocking them to the ground. A cloud of dust obscured Ronin’s optics as he twisted to search for Lara . Strong hands grasped his head, forcing him to face Warlord. The weight of at least three bots settled atop Ronin, pinning him to the ground, their hands and feet pressing on his limbs . “All this trouble for a meatbag,” Warlord said as the dust settled . “Fuck you,” Lara spat. She held an arm around her midsection . “You already did.” Warlord kicked her ribs. She cried out, tumbling over the dust. “You could have made this painless for her, dustwalker, if you had listened to me .” Warlord’s stride was unhurried as he walked to Lara. He crouched before her, taking a fistful of her hair, and lifted her head from the dirt. Lara bared her teeth, a muted sound of pain rising from her throat . “Maybe I’ll let the rest of them fuck her before she’s done. Find out why you thought she was worth keeping .” Ronin had been slow to recognize his growing love for Lara, but he was learning hatred much faster. Lara was his life, his reason, his long-sought-after purpose. And Warlord meant to take her away. She was going to be killed, and Ronin couldn’t do anything to stop it . This was no matter of survival for Warlord. That thing — neither bot nor human — had nothing to gain or lose in this, no motivation apart from prejudice, cruelty, and pride. Despite the contempt blossoming in him, Ronin could not understand Warlord’s hatred; it was directed at a people he used to belong to . Ronin’s ire had a singular target and clear reasons. It was an emotion of cold logic, and, had he known it sooner, he would have ended Warlord at his earliest opportunity . Lara lashed out, fingers digging into the sutured gash on Warlord’s face. She pulled, tearing the synthetic skin away from his jaw, revealing the metal plates beneath. He snapped his head to the side, ripping off more skin before her grip broke. When he turned his gaze on her, she spat in his face . Warlord’s open palm cracked against the side of her face. Blood sprayed from her mouth, gathering on grass and dirt in bright droplets. He slapped her again before she recovered . Ronin twisted beneath the gearheads, shifting his weight to throw them off-balance. Lara would be killed while he watched. He would never have her back, would never be able to touch her again or hear her voice directly, would never see the spark of life in her eyes or experience the surge of emotion roused by her dances . He pulled one of his arms free and latched it on the throat of the nearest gearhead. Metal crunched beneath his closing fingers, and he pulled. The bot’s head lolled back limply as Ronin tossed away the components of its neck . Some of the weight pinning him fell away, and he pushed himself up . Four shots rang out, echoing like thunder in the morning sky. Four points of pain exploded across Ronin’s torso. He fell to the ground on his stomach, systems reeling as they assessed the damage, alerts blaring across his processors . “Ronin!” Lara cried out, voice weak and strained . “Pull his fucking power cell,” Warlord said. “Let the Dust have him .” Gearheads pinned down his limbs, and new pain flared down Ronin’s back, sensory circuits broken as his skin was torn away. He registered strong force exerted on his casing, and a moment later an armored plate was pried loose . There was a crack, and Lara screamed. Ronin lifted his head, twisting his neck to see her curled in the dirt, blood trickling from her nose and mouth . “Lara!” he shouted . A hand clamped over his power cell . Ronin ceased to exist . * * * S omething flickered through the blackness, a spark of energy in a field of nothing. It pulsed across electrodes, rebooting systems one-by-one in its wake, spreading power throughout Ronin’s body. Diagnostics indicated five breaches of his casing —four on his back, and an exit hole on his chest . He opened his eyelids, but it took three seconds for his optics to come online. At first, there was only bright white, but a shadowy figure materialized as the cameras adjusted. Static hummed through the image, and finally his vision cleared . Newton was kneeling over Ronin, his mouth moving . Ronin’s audio receptors crackled on next, varying wildly between a distant, unintelligible drone and the overwhelming feedback from the wind before finally normalizing to reasonable levels . “—need to get up, Ronin!” Newton pleaded . Ronin nodded. The ground was no place for him, especially with his casing breached. But why was Newton so anxious about it? With Newton’s help, he rose into a sitting position, processors reviewing various data as it became available . This had happened before, though Ronin hadn’t known Newton’s name, then. Hadn’t claimed a name of his own . He accessed his most recent memories as systems powered up. He’d been traveling through the Dust, seeking shelter from a storm, and Lara — Pull his fucking energy cell . Ronin surged to his feet as the memories assailed him, every instant captured with unerring, terrifying detail . “Where is she?” he demanded, turning his head to search the immediate area. The only evidence of Warlord’s presence were the boot prints in the dirt. All of Ronin’s gear — rifle, packs of scrap, water, food, and ammunition — was gone . Newton adjusted his position, blocking Ronin’s view. “You will need to remain calm. There was nothing — ” Ronin shoved Newton aside and stumbled forward, falling to his knees beside the figure on the ground. Dark blood had splattered the dust beneath her, more of it drying in her hair and on her clothes. Her face was turned away . He reached forward to touch her arm, stopping when he saw the dark bruising around her elbow. “Lara,” he said, and her name was carried away on the wind . “She’s not dead. Not yet,” Newton said. “But…her arm is broken, her ribs are, at the very least, fractured, and she’s bruised in large patches of her torso and face. She could recover from all that, with proper and immediate treatment, but if she’s bleeding internally, it will kill her .” “You can diagnose her,” Ronin said, his voice flat, not looking away from Lara, “you can save her .” “Not I. I have some of the knowledge required, but none of the tools .” Gently, Ronin hooked her hair with his fingers and drew it away from her face. Her eyes were black and swollen shut, lips split and oozing blood. Weak breaths struggled through her open mouth . He could hardly recognize the woman he knew. The woman he loved . His hands fell to his sides, fingers curling to dig grooves in the dirt. He’d long expected to meet his end in the Dust, one day, but it wasn’t supposed to take her . Clenching his fists, he slammed them into the ground, again and again. The Dust had provided, and he’d walked it without complaint, taking the hardships as they came. It was his means of survival. His only purpose . It had no right to demand this price . He’d vowed to protect her , to — This was the world Newton had woken him up to. The first time, so long ago, Ronin had lost himself upon waking. Now, he had lost the only person he cared about in all the years since. Newton had reactivated him, but Lara had granted Ronin life . Was the bot standing behind him worthy of some of Ronin’s newfound hatred ? “I wish I could help,” Newton said . “You stood and watched .” “What good could I have done, Ronin? They’d have torn me apart, and neither of us would be active now .” “It might have allowed me enough time to end them. A chance to fight. A chance…to save her .” He lifted a hand again, lightly brushing his trembling fingers over her bloody cheek. How long before her skin went cold ? “You have a chance to avenge her, hollow as that is, back in Cheyenne .” In Cheyenne. Could Ronin bring himself to go near that place again? Could he bear to walk the same paths he’d walked with Lara, to be reminded of her with each step? Back there , where — “You said you do not have the tools to save her.” Ronin looked over his shoulder, fixing an optic on Newton . “Correct.” Newton tilted his head, brow plates shifting down and drawing together. “The Air Force Base .” “ Do they ?” “They did, when I was last there. It’s been many years …” “Will she survive the journey?” The answer didn’t matter. If it was her only chance, he’d take her there, regardless. If she were to die, it would be in his arms as he attempted to save her. It would break him, all the same … “I don’t know. Time is of the essence, but she is in a delicate state. You must hurry , and — ” “ We must hurry. You’re coming .” “I-I…I can be of no — ” “You had a part in shaping this world, Newton, regardless of your intentions. You can’t hide away and pretend it doesn’t exist .” Ronin leaned forward, carefully slipping one arm beneath Lara’s knees and the other under her shoulders and neck. He lifted her off the ground and stood. Her body hung limp, broken arm folded over her midsection and the other dangling . He turned to Newton and met his optics . “This is who I am. I will not stand here and wait for her to die. Have you remembered who you are? Get me into that base, so we can save her .” Newton was silent as precious seconds ticked by. The wind sighed over the hills, carrying loose dirt that had ridden the storm. Finally, he nodded . “Let’s go, Ronin .”
